UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores core user profile information and application-wide preferences. This entity consolidates data from Firestore paths like 'users/{uid}' and 'users/{uid}/prefs/main'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

lastSeen.description: Timestamp of the user's last activity or login.

modeDefault.description: The default 'mode' (e.g., 'Minimal', 'Focus') selected for the user on initial login, influencing the initial dashboard view.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the user preferences schema, used for managing data migrations and ensuring compatibility across app updates.

visualProfile.description: User's preferred visual settings (e.g., 'density', 'motion', 'contrast', 'font scaling') affecting the UI's appearance.

lastMigratedAt.description: Timestamp of when the user's core profile or preferences data was last migrated to a newer schema version.

UserLayout.title: UserLayout
UserLayout.description: Stores user-defined dashboard layouts, including panel configurations and grid settings. This corresponds to the Firestore path 'users/{uid}/layouts/{layoutId}'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserLayout entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that owns this layout. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserLayout)

modeId.description: The ID of the mode this layout is associated with (e.g., 'focusMode', 'minimalMode', 'proDeskMode').

name.description: A user-friendly name or identifier for the custom layout, allowing users to distinguish between their saved layouts.

panelConfig.description: An array of objects, where each object describes the configuration of a single panel within the dashboard (e.g., chart type, data source, stable ID, specific settings).

gridConfig.description: A serialized configuration string or object defining the layout, positioning, and sizing of panels within the dashboard grid.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the layout schema, used for managing data migrations and ensuring compatibility when updating layout structures.

lastMigratedAt.description: Timestamp of when this specific layout's data was last migrated to a newer schema version.

ExperimentAssignment.title: ExperimentAssignment
ExperimentAssignment.description: Records a user's assignment to a specific variant in an A/B experiment or feature flag test. Corresponds to the Firestore path 'experiments/{uid}'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExperimentAssignment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity to which this assignment belongs. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ExperimentAssignment)

experimentName.description: The name of the experiment or feature flag (e.g., 'onboarding_variant', 'enable_pro_mode').

assignmentValue.description: The specific value or variant assigned to the user for this experiment (e.g., 'workflow', 'brain UI', 'enabled', 'disabled').

assignedAt.description: Timestamp when the user was assigned to this experiment variant.

Feedback.title: Feedback
Feedback.description: Stores user-submitted feedback, bug reports, or identified 'confusion points' for application improvement. Corresponds to the Firestore path 'feedback/{uid_event}'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Feedback entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who submitted this feedback. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Feedback)

description.description: Detailed description of the feedback, issue, or user's confusion.

eventContext.description: Optional, contextual data (e.g., URL, mode, panel state, error messages) about where the feedback was given, aiding reproduction or understanding.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the feedback was submitted.

UserEvent.title: UserEvent
UserEvent.description: Logs user-specific events, such as onboarding progress, mode selections, and key interactions for analytics and understanding user behavior. Corresponds to the Firestore path 'users/{uid}/events/{eventId}'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserEvent entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity associated with this event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserEvent)

eventType.description: The category or type of event (e.g., 'onboarding_step_completed', 'mode_selected', 'panel_interaction', 'feature_accessed').

eventDetails.description: A JSON string containing additional details pertinent to the event, specific to the eventType (e.g., '{ "step": 1 }' for onboarding, or '{ "mode": "Minimal" }' for mode selection).

definition.description: Stores core user profile information, application-wide preferences (like modeDefault and visualProfile), and user roles. Access is strictly controlled by the 'userId' in the path, ensuring only the authenticated user can read/write their own profile. Includes consolidated preferences data originally suggested for 'users/{uid}/prefs/main'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication User ID (UID).

definition.description: Contains user-defined dashboard layouts, including panel and grid configurations. Ownership is established by the hierarchical path, where '{userId}' in the path must match 'request.auth.uid'. The 'userId' field within the document is denormalized for data integrity and query flexibility, though redundant for path-based rule authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this layout.

params.name: layoutId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user-defined layout.

definition.description: Logs user-specific events and interactions for analytics. Access is strictly controlled by the 'userId' in the path, ensuring only the authenticated user can read/write their own events. The 'userId' field within the document is denormalized for data integrity and query flexibility, though redundant for path-based rule authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user associated with this event.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user event.

definition.description: Records a user's assignment to an A/B experiment or feature flag. This collection is secured by checking the denormalized 'userId' field within the document against 'request.auth.uid'. This enables authorization independence, allowing direct reads/writes without 'get()' operations, and supports QAPs for secure list queries.

params.name: experimentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific experiment assignment.

definition.description: Stores user-submitted feedback, bug reports, or 'confusion points'. This collection is secured by checking the denormalized 'userId' field within the document against 'request.auth.uid'. This design ensures authorization independence, allowing atomic operations and supporting QAPs for secure list queries.

params.name: feedbackId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific feedback submission.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable by adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. The primary goal is to achieve **Authorization Independence** and support **Query-as-a-Policy (QAP)** for secure list operations.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**

1.  **Path-Based Ownership:** For entities like `UserProfile`, `UserLayout`, and `UserEvent`, the authorization context is inherently managed by the document path. For instance, `/users/{userId}` ensures that `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}` to access a user's profile. Similarly, subcollections like `/users/{userId}/layouts/{layoutId}` and `/users/{userId}/events/{eventId}` securely scope access to the authenticated user's data. This eliminates the need for `get()` operations in security rules to check parent documents for ownership, making rules atomic, simpler to write, and easier to debug.

2.  **Document-Level Denormalization:** For top-level collections that still represent user-owned data, such as `ExperimentAssignment` (`/experiments/{experimentId}`) and `Feedback` (`/feedback/{feedbackId}`), the `userId` field is explicitly denormalized and stored within each document. This means security rules can directly check `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` without performing any `get()` operations to other collections or parent documents. This design is critical for maintaining authorization independence and ensuring consistent security checks across different data structures.

**Query-as-a-Policy (QAPs):**

1.  **Secure List Operations for Hierarchical Data:** For subcollections like `/users/{userId}/layouts` and `/users/{userId}/events`, list operations are inherently secure because the `userId` wildcard in the path *must* match `request.auth.uid`. A user can only list their own layouts or events, preventing unauthorized access to other users' data.

2.  **Enforced Filtering for Top-Level User-Owned Data:** For collections like `/experiments` and `/feedback`, where documents are stored at the root level but owned by a specific user, security rules will enforce `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`. This means any client-side `list` query on these collections *must* include a `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)` clause. If a query attempts to list all documents without this filter, the query will be denied by the rules, thereby enforcing QAP and preventing broad, unauthorized data leakage.

**Structural Segregation:** Each collection maintains a homogeneous security posture. All documents within `/users/{userId}/layouts` are user-owned layouts, and all documents within `/experiments` are user-owned experiment assignments. There is no mixing of public, private, or collaborative data within the same collection, simplifying security rules significantly.

This design prioritizes explicit ownership, atomic authorization checks, and secure query patterns, providing a robust foundation for InsightFlow's data management.
